title: Donna And Joe

jokes.text: Because orchids are Donna's favorite flowers, and she's my favorite flower.
jokes.setup: Joe explains why they're having orchids at the wedding
jokes.punchline: Comparing Donna to a flower in a sweet but cheesy way
jokes.explanation: Sweet romantic cheese that works because it's genuinely endearing rather than trying too hard

dialogue.speaker: Joe
dialogue.line: We're having orchids at the wedding because, like an orchid, Donna is exotic, rare, and absolutely beautiful.

dialogue.speaker: Joe
dialogue.line: Also like an orchid, she requires very specific conditions to thrive, and if you mess up even slightly, she will kill you.

jokes.text: Ha ha! We real cute.
jokes.setup: Joe's cheesy romantic comment
jokes.punchline: Donna's self-aware acknowledgment of their cuteness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna response - confident, self-aware, and economical delivery

dialogue.speaker: Joe
dialogue.line: You know, I've been thinking about us, and I realized that we're actually pretty cute together.

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: Yeah, we are. And I'm gonna enjoy this before one of us dies or realizes what a terrible mistake this is.

jokes.text: Do you think this is my first time working a side job as a wedding planner for a former co-worker?
jokes.setup: Joe asking if April knows about wedding planning
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific scenario April presents as if it's common
jokes.explanation: Classic April deadpan delivery of an absurd premise as if it's completely normal

dialogue.speaker: Joe
dialogue.line: Do you know anything about wedding planning?

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Well, I once organized a funeral for a possum that died in my garage, so yes, I know everything about wedding planning.

jokes.text: I mostly have my rage problem under control, but planning is very stressful.
jokes.setup: April apologizing for her outburst
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning she has a 'rage problem'
jokes.explanation: April's matter-of-fact delivery of something alarming is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I'm sorry for my outburst. I have a rage problem.

jokes.text: Please avoid my trigger words, 'flowers,' 'schedule,' 'vows,' 'bride,' 'groom,' 'food,' 'love,' 'happy,' 'church,' 'event,' 'wedding,' and 'Craig.'
jokes.setup: April warning about trigger words
jokes.punchline: The list includes basically everything related to weddings, plus Craig's name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that builds to the perfect punchline of Craig just being a trigger word himself

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I'm warning you, there are some trigger words. The list includes basically everything related to weddings, plus Craig's name.

jokes.text: I am so sorry, Craig. I'm sorry, I'm sorry.
jokes.setup: April just said Craig's name is a trigger word
jokes.punchline: Immediately having to apologize to Craig for saying his name
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback that pays off the previous joke

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, Craig. I didn't mean to say your name.

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: It's okay, April. I know it was an accident.

jokes.text: And I love you like a sister. - Ew.
jokes.setup: Donna expressing genuine affection
jokes.punchline: April's disgusted reaction to emotional sentiment
jokes.explanation: Perfect April response - one word that completely deflates the sentiment while being true to character

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: You know what? I care about you. You're like a sister to me.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Gross. Why would you say that? Now I have to go rinse my ears out.

jokes.text: I told your brother LeVondrious that he is not welcome.
jokes.setup: April handling wedding logistics
jokes.punchline: The absurdly elaborate name 'LeVondrious'
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from the over-the-top name, sets up LeVondrious for later payoff

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I need to name the centerpieces something ridiculous. How about LeVondrious?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: That's not a centerpiece name, that's a person's name.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Exactly. It's perfect.

jokes.text: I actually care whether you're having a good time and are happy. It's weird.
jokes.setup: April explaining why she'll handle the family drama
jokes.punchline: April being disturbed by her own capacity for caring
jokes.explanation: Classic April - her character growth expressed through her discomfort with said growth

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I'll handle the family drama. I'm very good at compartmentalizing emotions and weaponizing them against people I love.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Which is why it disturbs me that I actually care about you guys.

jokes.text: The Meagles are a cold-blooded crew of judgmental grudge-holders.
jokes.setup: Donna describing her family
jokes.punchline: The dramatic description of family dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the family chaos, alliteration helps the delivery

jokes.text: My cousin Winnie once forgot to use a coaster at my grandpa's house, and he wrote her out of his will.
jokes.setup: Explaining how petty the Meagles are
jokes.punchline: The extreme overreaction to a minor coaster infraction
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of pettiness - relatable setup with absurd consequences

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: The Meagles are so petty. One time at a country club, Ben's ball landed on their cart path. A CART PATH. You know what they did? They called the club president, demanded we pay for a new cart, and made us sit at a different table for dinner.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: That's insane.

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: Wait, there's more. The coaster from my drink left a ring on their glass table. Just a small ring. Barely visible. Gayle screamed like someone had murdered her dog. They hired a furniture restoration specialist. A specialist. For a coaster ring. The bill was $800.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Did they make you pay?

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: Of course they did. And then they sent us a framed photo of the restored table with a note that said 'Please be more careful next time.' Next time! Like I was going back to their house voluntarily.

jokes.text: The last four Meagle family Pictionary tournaments ended at the hospital.
jokes.setup: Continuing to describe Meagle dysfunction
jokes.punchline: Pictionary games resulting in hospitalization
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor - turning a benign family game into violence

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: One time we played Pictionary and my uncle drew something that looked like a hot dog. My cousin thought it was a gun and tackled him through a glass coffee table. He needed 47 stitches.

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: That's a Meagle family tradition.

jokes.text: Legally, no more than three Meagles are allowed on an international flight together.
jokes.setup: Final example of Meagle chaos
jokes.punchline: The family is so problematic they're legally restricted from flying
jokes.explanation: Peak escalation of the Meagle chaos - implies international incidents and legal intervention

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: The family is so problematic they're legally restricted from flying.

jokes.text: But they give great gifts. Gotta get that flatware.
jokes.setup: All the terrible things about the Meagles
jokes.punchline: Donna's mercenary motivation for tolerating them
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna pragmatism - completely undercuts the family drama with material concerns

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: The Meagles are terrible people. But you know what? They pay me in cash, they never ask questions, and they tip like they're trying to make a dead relative proud. So I tolerate them.

jokes.text: Uh, yes. Warm it up, please.
jokes.setup: Tommy being offered hand cream
jokes.punchline: His precious demand for it to be warmed
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom vanity and high maintenance behavior

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Wait, wait. Is that hand cream? Warm it up first.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I don't put anything on my hands that isn't at least 98.6 degrees.

jokes.text: As Donna's Butler of Honor, I'm going all out. Baby blue brocade.
jokes.setup: Tom describing his role in the wedding
jokes.punchline: 'Butler of Honor' instead of 'Man of Honor'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that fits Tom's pretentious vocabulary while being endearing

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I'm not the Best Man, I'm the Butler of Honor.

jokes.text: As a surprise, I got a matching dress for Lucy.
jokes.setup: Tom planning a surprise for Lucy
jokes.punchline: Buying her a dress without consulting her
jokes.explanation: Sets up Tom's romantic misstep - well-intentioned but presumptuous

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I'm going to surprise Lucy with a dress I picked out myself.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Tom, that's a terrible idea.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Why? I have great taste.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: You're wearing a shirt with a picture of a boat on it.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: It's a very nice boat.

jokes.text: And do you believe Lucy will be happy with this gift?
jokes.setup: Tom describing his surprise dress purchase
jokes.punchline: Ron's diplomatic way of questioning this terrible idea
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron delivery - polite but clearly skeptical, setting up the conflict

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Why would you do that?

jokes.text: 100%! We're so in sync.
jokes.setup: Ron questioning if Lucy will like the surprise dress
jokes.punchline: Tom's complete confidence despite obvious red flags
jokes.explanation: Tom's obliviousness to relationship dynamics creates dramatic irony

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Tom, are you sure Lucy is going to like this dress?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Ron, Ron, Ron. I have never been more confident about anything in my life. This dress is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: It's a size 0 and she's clearly a size 8.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: She'll grow into it.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: It's neon green.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: It's retro.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: There's a stain on it.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Vintage patina.

jokes.text: I finally feel like I'm with the person I'm gonna be with forever. I'd marry her tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Tom talking about being in sync with Lucy
jokes.punchline: Jumping to marriage after buying her a dress without asking
jokes.explanation: Sets up Tom's romantic overreach that will cause problems later

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: We're so in sync, I bought her a dress without asking and she loved it.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Next thing I know, we're getting married.

jokes.text: I love weddings. Good food, the celebration of romantic love, and most of all, churches.
jokes.setup: Ron listing what he loves about weddings
jokes.punchline: Churches being more important to him than romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron priorities - architecture over emotions

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I love weddings. Mostly the churches. I love the smell of the wood, the beautiful stained glass, the cool marble floors. I love kneeling, the sitting, the standing, the kneeling again. But mostly I love the kneeling.

jokes.text: Say what you will about organized religion, those bastards knew how to construct an edifice.
jokes.setup: Ron praising churches
jokes.punchline: Calling religious people 'bastards' while praising their architecture
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron - irreverent toward religion but respectful of craftsmanship

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I love the outdoors. And I hate most people. But I love their churches. Beautiful buildings. It's too bad they're full of bastards.

jokes.text: Do you think I could have 30 seconds? That's all.
jokes.setup: Roz enthusiastically talking about activities with the kids
jokes.punchline: Desperately asking for just 30 seconds of quiet
jokes.explanation: Relatable parent/caregiver exhaustion, the small request makes it funnier

dialogue.speaker: Roz
dialogue.line: The kids and I had such a great time today! We went to the park, then we got ice cream, then we played games, then we sang songs, then we danced, then we played more games!

dialogue.speaker: Roz
dialogue.line: Please. I'm begging you. Just 30 seconds of quiet. That's all I need.

jokes.text: She's fading.
jokes.setup: Roz asking for 30 seconds of quiet
jokes.punchline: Describing Roz like a dying person
jokes.explanation: Great economy and medical terminology for childcare exhaustion

dialogue.speaker: Roz
dialogue.line: I need thirty seconds of quiet.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Okay. And... go.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: It's like watching a dying person try to remember the name of their first dog.

jokes.text: I love you more than Ben.
jokes.setup: Leslie praising Roz as a caregiver
jokes.punchline: Declaring she loves the nanny more than her husband
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie over-the-top appreciation, shows her priorities as a working mom

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Roz, you're such an amazing caregiver. Honestly, I love you more than I love Ben.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I'm just saying, you're really great with the kids.

jokes.text: If Ben left me, I would be sad, but I would get through it. But if you left me, I would never recover.
jokes.setup: Leslie saying she loves Roz more than Ben
jokes.punchline: Explaining in detail how losing Roz would be worse than losing her husband
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that doubles down on the joke while feeling authentic to Leslie's character

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I love Roz more than Ben. If I lost Roz, I would have no one to complain to about Ben. I need her.

jokes.text: I'm so used to packing for them I almost put in diapers for myself.
jokes.setup: Leslie packing for the hotel trip
jokes.punchline: Almost packing diapers for herself out of habit
jokes.explanation: Relatable parent humor about being on autopilot

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Almost packing diapers for myself out of habit.

jokes.text: The whole world is my park.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining why she loves her national parks job
jokes.punchline: Her grandiose way of describing her work scope
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Leslie's idealistic worldview and love of her work

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I love my job because I get to protect America's greatest natural treasures. Every day, I wake up knowing that I'm preserving these magnificent landscapes for future generations. I'm not just a parks department employee—I'm a guardian of the American spirit, a steward of the land, a—

jokes.text: All three of them just bumped into each other and broke everything you own.
jokes.setup: Ben saying things aren't that chaotic
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction as the kids cause mayhem
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom timing - statement immediately contradicted by events

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Things aren't that chaotic.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: We've got everything under control.

dialogue.speaker: Child 1
dialogue.line: Ahhhhh!

dialogue.speaker: Child 2
dialogue.line: Help!

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Never mind.

jokes.text: I--I don't know what that was.
jokes.setup: Kids causing chaos
jokes.punchline: Ben's helpless confusion at the destruction
jokes.explanation: Standard parent bewilderment, functional but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: What happened here? Why is there a hole in the wall?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: And why are all the books on the floor? And why is the couch upside down?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I was gone for twenty minutes. Twenty minutes!

jokes.text: You know Gayle gives me a gift each and every day. Oh, I got the greatest gift of all. Being married to you.
jokes.setup: Jerry responding to discussion of gifts
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top romantic sentiment about Gayle
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry wholesomeness that's both endearing and cringe-worthy

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Gayle is the most beautiful, intelligent, talented woman I have ever known. She makes me want to be a better man. Every day with her is like a gift from God himself.

jokes.text: Control yourselves, Gergiches!
jokes.setup: Jerry and Gayle being romantic
jokes.punchline: Tom treating their affection like it's obscene
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Tom being dramatic about normal displays of affection

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! Hey! There are children present!

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I'm calling the police. This is indecent exposure.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Get a room, you two. Better yet, get a bunker.

jokes.text: Tom is quite taken with you. He said he'd marry you tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Ron talking to Lucy about Tom's feelings
jokes.punchline: Ron immediately revealing Tom's marriage comment
jokes.explanation: Ron's blunt honesty creating relationship drama, perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Tom said he wants to get married.

dialogue.speaker: Lucy
dialogue.line: Really? That's surprising.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Yes. He told me in confidence, but I'm telling you immediately because I don't respect his privacy.

jokes.text: What's that horrible sound? - Children. - Ugh, your life is gross.
jokes.setup: Jen hearing noise in the background
jokes.punchline: Her disgusted reaction to learning it's children
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jen Barkley character moment - complete disdain for family life

dialogue.speaker: Jen
dialogue.line: What is that noise in the background?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Oh, those are my kids. They're playing.

dialogue.speaker: Jen
dialogue.line: Children? Ugh.

jokes.text: You guys live in Pawnee, Indiana. Where do you have to go? Putting up a new scarecrow?
jokes.setup: Jen questioning where they need to go
jokes.punchline: Her condescending assumption about small-town life
jokes.explanation: Classic city person's stereotypical view of rural life, fits Jen's character

dialogue.speaker: Jen
dialogue.line: Where do we need to go?

dialogue.speaker: Jen
dialogue.line: Oh, I assume there's a town square where everyone gathers to discuss harvests and compare yeoman accomplishments?

jokes.text: We're talking about the House of Representatives, Ben. You might be overqualified.
jokes.setup: Ben saying he's not qualified for Congress
jokes.punchline: Jen suggesting he's overqualified instead
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about the low bar for congressional competence

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I'm not qualified for Congress.

dialogue.speaker: Jen
dialogue.line: Actually, I think you're overqualified.

jokes.text: Two of the three kids are showering in their pajamas, and most of Ben's ties are in the toilet.
jokes.setup: Leslie updating on the chaos at home
jokes.punchline: The absurd specific details of the destruction
jokes.explanation: Good specific details that paint a picture of complete domestic chaos

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: The absurd specific details of the destruction

jokes.text: I feel like there should be a term for that. Like, 'nicey-meanie.'
jokes.setup: April describing the Meagles' passive-aggressive behavior
jokes.punchline: Her childish made-up term 'nicey-meanie'
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - creating a juvenile term for something sophisticated like passive-aggression

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: They're so passive-aggressive. They do this thing where they're nice to your face but then they're mean behind your back. I call it being a 'nicey-meanie.'

jokes.text: Or woman. I mean, equal rights. No, that doesn't apply.
jokes.setup: Ben considering being a congressman
jokes.punchline: His confused attempt at gender-inclusive language
jokes.explanation: Ben's awkward political correctness, shows his nervousness about the position

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: You know, I've been thinking about running for Congress. I want to make sure I'm inclusive and represent everyone. So I'm going to start saying 'congressperson' instead of 'congressman.'

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: That's great, Ben!

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Yeah, and I'm going to say 'chairwoman' or 'chairperson' depending on the situation.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Very thoughtful.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I'm also going to refer to my constituents as 'you guys and dolls.'

jokes.text: Ben should be the Royal Archduke Sultan Emperor of All Inhabitable Lands on Earth! I'm completely neutral.
jokes.setup: Leslie claiming to be neutral about Ben running
jokes.punchline: Her completely over-the-top endorsement followed by claiming neutrality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Leslie moment - the absurd title contrasted with claiming neutrality is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I'm completely neutral about Ben running for city council. I have no opinion whatsoever.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: But if he does run, he's going to be amazing. He's the smartest, most talented, most attractive man in the entire world. Vote for Ben Wyatt!

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: But like I said, I'm neutral.

jokes.text: Don't call me 'man.'
jokes.setup: Tom calling Ron 'man' while asking for help
jokes.punchline: Ron's specific objection to being called 'man'
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron formality and dignity, even in casual conversation

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Ron, man, I need your help with something.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Don't call me 'man.' I'm not your man. I'm a free citizen with my own name.

jokes.text: That's what you took away from this?
jokes.setup: Tom spilling his heart about his relationship fears
jokes.punchline: Ron only caring about being called 'man'
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic - Tom's exasperation at Ron's priorities

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I'm scared, man. What if this doesn't work out? What if I'm not good enough for her?

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: You called me 'man.' We're good.

jokes.text: Don't serve this man. No more food until he fixes his giant disaster he's created.
jokes.setup: Tom's relationship problems
jokes.punchline: Ron withholding food as punishment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron - using food as leverage, treating relationship drama like serious business

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: If Tom doesn't straighten up, I'm cutting off his food supply. A man needs to learn consequences.

jokes.text: Nope, too hard.
jokes.setup: Jen telling Ben to practice meet and greet
jokes.punchline: Ben immediately giving up after seeing the first people
jokes.explanation: Ben's social anxiety and lack of natural political instincts

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Hi, I'm Ben Wyatt. I'm running for City Council.

dialogue.speaker: Voter
dialogue.line: We don't care.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Okay, well, I'm just going to go home and lie in bed and think about this.

jokes.text: Well, I hope that you are a podiatrist or a dentist, because I seem to have a case of foot-in-mouth disorder.
jokes.setup: Ben making multiple mistakes meeting Donna's parents
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt at a self-deprecating medical pun
jokes.explanation: Dad joke level humor that fits Ben's awkwardness but isn't particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Your daughter is amazing, and I promise to take care of her. Also, I think I'm having a stroke because I can't feel my face.

jokes.text: Here are all the troublemakers, boss.
jokes.setup: Andy helping April manage the Meagles
jokes.punchline: Treating family members like criminals to be rounded up
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment - approaching family dynamics like law enforcement

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Okay, so we're treating family members like criminals to be rounded up?

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: That's the plan.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I love it. It's like a fun, family-oriented police state.

jokes.text: And, Ginuwine? - Yes? - Get it together.
jokes.setup: April managing the Meagle family drama
jokes.punchline: Addressing someone named Ginuwine with complete seriousness
jokes.explanation: The absurd name Ginuwine (like the R&B singer) delivered with April's deadpan authority

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I need you to understand something, Ginuwine. I don't care about your feuds with the other Meagles. I don't care about your drama. What I care about is that you show up on time, do your job, and don't make my life harder than it already is.

dialogue.speaker: Ginuwine
dialogue.line: But April—

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I'm not done. You're going to sit down, shut up, and listen to what I'm telling you right now, because if you don't, I will make sure that every single member of your family regrets the day they were born.

jokes.text: Easy peasy. Lemon sneezy.
jokes.setup: April successfully managing the family chaos
jokes.punchline: Her childish variation on 'easy peasy lemon squeezy'
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - competent at the job but still maintaining her quirky language

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Easy peasy lemon squeezy.

jokes.text: I am a liar. Well, no, I am not a liar. I have never lied about anything in my life. Though I suppose you could construe camouflage as a lie.
jokes.setup: Tom trying to correct what Ron said to Lucy
jokes.punchline: His confusing self-contradictory explanation about lying
jokes.explanation: Tom's nervous babbling when trying to backtrack, the camouflage detail is oddly specific

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: No, no, no, Lucy, what Ron meant to say is that I don't lie. I just tell alternative facts. There's a difference. Lying is when you say something that's not true. Alternative facts are when you say something that's not true, but you say it with more confidence.

jokes.text: Wait, now we're on an island? With kids? Plural?
jokes.setup: Ron explaining Tom's comments about marriage and island life
jokes.punchline: Lucy's progressive alarm at each new detail
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Lucy's concern as Tom's fantasy life gets more elaborate

jokes.text: Watch out for those riding lawnmowers, man.
jokes.setup: Conversation about Tom's relationship problems
jokes.punchline: Andy's completely random and irrelevant warning
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy non-sequitur that comes from nowhere and has nothing to do with anything

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Hey, just a heads up — if you ever see a possum in your house, don't try to pet it. They have opposable thumbs and they will steal your wallet.

jokes.text: I feel "condifent." - Con... I feel "condifent."
jokes.setup: Leslie being drunk and trying to say 'confident'
jokes.punchline: Repeatedly mispronouncing the word despite trying to correct herself
jokes.explanation: Good drunk physical comedy showing Leslie's rare loss of control

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I'm very... confibent. No wait, confident. Con-fi-dent. Confibent. I meant confident!

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Why is this word so hard? Con-FI-dent. Confibent. Ugh, confident!

jokes.text: I feel 'condifent.' Con... I feel 'condifent.'
jokes.setup: Leslie being drunk from one sip of wine
jokes.punchline: Her inability to pronounce 'confident' correctly
jokes.explanation: Standard drunk humor, works because Leslie is usually so articulate

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I'm feeling very... confadent. No wait, confidant. Con-fi-dent. Confadent.

jokes.text: What? It's you! I love you! You're my sexy roommate. We love each other.
jokes.setup: Ben giving a wedding toast while drunk
jokes.punchline: His overly intimate description of marriage as sexy roommates
jokes.explanation: Ben's drunk earnestness about marriage being essentially roommates with benefits

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Marriage is like having a best friend who you can also have sex with. You're just sexy roommates. Sexy roommates who file taxes together and occasionally argue about whose turn it is to take out the garbage.

jokes.text: Whoo! He's talking about me.
jokes.setup: Ben's romantic toast about marriage
jokes.punchline: Leslie's proud drunk declaration that she's the sexy roommate
jokes.explanation: Sweet drunk Leslie pride in being Ben's subject

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I'm the sexy roommate!

jokes.text: And I am Ben Wyatt, and I very much approve this message!
jokes.setup: Ben finishing his wedding toast
jokes.punchline: Using political campaign language at a wedding
jokes.explanation: Good blend of Ben being drunk and thinking about his potential campaign

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Leslie, when I first met you, I thought you were crazy. You had all these wild ideas and no filter. But over the years, I realized that's what I love about you. You see the world not as it is, but as it could be. So today, I'm asking the people in this room to join me in voting for Leslie Knope for wife of the year. And I promise you, together, we can make our marriage great again.

jokes.text: Let the little man dance.
jokes.setup: April asking if she should stop Ben's dancing
jokes.punchline: Ron's condescending but affectionate description of Ben
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron - paternal amusement at Ben's rare moment of letting loose

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Let the man dance. He's like a young, gangly foal learning to use his legs for the first time. It's beautiful, really.

jokes.text: I also called 867-5309 100 times.
jokes.setup: Leslie remembering what she did while drunk
jokes.punchline: Calling the number from the Tommy Tutone song repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Good drunk behavior detail that references pop culture in a believable way

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I called 867-5309.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Over and over again.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: All night.

jokes.text: I want to come out strong on education. Then I'll tack hard into fiscal responsibility.
jokes.setup: Ben's drunk voicemails to Jen
jokes.punchline: Him using serious political strategy language while drunk
jokes.explanation: Ben's nerdy personality showing through even when drunk - policy wonk to the core

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Jen, hey, it's Ben. Look, I've been thinking about us, and I realize now that we need to pivot our messaging strategy to realign with core demographics and synergize our key value propositions.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Also, I'm drunk and I miss you. We should leverage this moment to restructure our relationship framework going forward.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Call me back. Let's touch base about our vertical integration.

jokes.text: ♪ 8-6-7-5-3-0-9 ♪ I love that song. ♪ Jenny, I've got your number ♪
jokes.setup: Leslie's earlier admission about calling the song number
jokes.punchline: Hearing them drunkenly sing the song together
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows them both being drunk and nostalgic

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I may have called and requested it.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: You what?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I called the bar and requested our song. I wanted to hear it.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Leslie, that's so romantic. And also completely insane.

jokes.text: I'm just gonna go spend my time doing exactly what I want to do, because I don't have children.
jokes.setup: Jen dealing with Ben and Leslie's parenting chaos
jokes.punchline: Her smug declaration about her child-free lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jen Barkley exit - rubbing her freedom in their faces

jokes.text: I'll do whatever you want. I'm fine with whatever. No. Whatever you want. No pressure.
jokes.setup: Ron talking to the church historian
jokes.punchline: Ron being uncharacteristically awkward and deferential
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Ron losing his usual confidence when meeting an expert in something he loves

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, I didn't mean to interrupt. Please, continue with what you were saying. I'm genuinely interested in hearing your thoughts on this matter.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: You know, I really respect your expertise here. I wouldn't want to overstep or impose my views. What would you recommend?

jokes.text: He was.
jokes.setup: Donna saying Michelle's boyfriend wasn't into her
jokes.punchline: Michelle's blunt confirmation that he was
jokes.explanation: Brutally efficient comedy - two words that completely undercut Donna's reassurance

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: Your boyfriend wasn't really into you.

dialogue.speaker: Michelle
dialogue.line: Yeah, he wasn't.

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Typhoon.
jokes.setup: Someone new appearing during the wedding prep chaos
jokes.punchline: The completely absurd name 'Typhoon'
jokes.explanation: Simple name humor - Typhoon is an absurd human name

dialogue.line: The completely absurd name 'Typhoon'

jokes.text: Typhoon, I am interested, but now is not the time.
jokes.setup: Typhoon introducing themselves
jokes.punchline: April's matter-of-fact acknowledgment that she's interested in whatever Typhoon offers
jokes.explanation: Perfect April response - intrigued by chaos but focused on her job

dialogue.speaker: Typhoon
dialogue.line: I am Typhoon, a spiritual guide and healer.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Cool. I'm interested.

jokes.text: Ron does this weird thing where he says exactly what he means.
jokes.setup: Ron giving sincere advice about relationships
jokes.punchline: Tom describing honesty as a weird quirk
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observation about Ron's character and commentary on how unusual honesty is

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: The key to a good relationship is honesty. You have to be completely honest with your partner.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Honesty? That's like... a weird quirk of yours, right?

jokes.text: Well, I'm only dating you for the free dresses, so...
jokes.setup: Tom's sincere declaration of love and commitment
jokes.punchline: Lucy's sarcastic deflection about materialistic motives
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the dress situation while deflecting Tom's intensity with humor

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Lucy, I love you. I want to spend the rest of my life with you.

dialogue.speaker: Lucy
dialogue.line: Aww, that's sweet. Is this before or after you liquidate my assets?

jokes.text: Just yesterday, I learned that the Pearl Jam album, Vitalogy was written about you.
jokes.setup: Joe's wedding vows about learning new things about Donna
jokes.punchline: The absurd claim about a famous album being about her
jokes.explanation: Creative wedding vow humor that plays on Donna's larger-than-life personality

dialogue.speaker: Joe
dialogue.line: I've learned so many new things about you, Donna. Like, did you know that Fleetwood Mac's 'Rumours' album was actually written about you?

jokes.text: Tell the kids not to worry, they're coming back home.
jokes.setup: Leslie on the phone with Roz about zebras
jokes.punchline: The implication that live zebras were somehow involved with the kids
jokes.explanation: Completely absurd escalation of the childcare chaos - zebras appearing out of nowhere

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Hi Roz, it's Leslie Knope from Parks and Rec. I'm calling because I heard there was an incident at the DMV with some zebras and children?

dialogue.speaker: Roz
dialogue.line: Yeah, that's right. Some zebras got loose in the waiting area.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Oh my God. Were the children okay? I mean, were they... interacting with the zebras?

dialogue.speaker: Roz
dialogue.line: Unfortunately, yes.

jokes.text: And I'm holding zebras.
jokes.setup: Reporters asking Ben about his qualifications
jokes.punchline: Ben's exasperated reference to his current zebra situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows how absurd their life is - using zebras as an excuse to reporters

dialogue.speaker: Reporter
dialogue.line: What are your qualifications for this position?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Well, I've worked in government for over a decade. I've balanced budgets, managed crises, and I've even dealt with a current zebra situation that I really don't want to get into.

jokes.text: To me. I own my own restaurant and several other properties. That's always worth celebrating.
jokes.setup: Wedding toasts to the happy couple and Ben's campaign
jokes.punchline: Tom making the toast about his business success instead
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom narcissism - turning any celebration into self-promotion

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I just want to say, Leslie and Ben, you two are perfect together. You complement each other. But I have to be honest, I'm not here to talk about your love. I'm here to talk about my business success. Rent-A-Swag has really taken off. We're in three malls now. Three! I've also started a second company called Entertainment 720, which is going to revolutionize the way people think about entertainment. Ben, your campaign is great, but let me tell you about my business plan...

jokes.text: Ha ha. That's your new name. Garry.
jokes.setup: Jerry being called by his actual name for once
jokes.punchline: Tom deciding to call him Garry as a new variation
jokes.explanation: Continuation of the Jerry/Larry/Terry running gag with a new variation

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Wait, did someone just call him Jerry? His actual name?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: You know what, I'm going to call him Garry from now on. It's like Jerry, but with a G. It's a power move.

jokes.text: Yes, after 30 years, my coworkers are finally going to call me by my real name. Oh, boy, I'm blessed.
jokes.setup: Everyone calling Jerry 'Garry'
jokes.punchline: Jerry's sincere gratitude for being called the wrong name again
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry moment - being grateful even when still not getting the respect he deserves

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Thank you. Thank you so much for calling me Garry again. It means the world to me.

jokes.text: Didn't expect to see your baby bro at your wedding, huh? Well, I'm here... despite what you did to me all those years ago.
jokes.setup: LeVondrious arriving after being banned
jokes.punchline: Dramatic entrance with vague accusation
jokes.explanation: Good callback with typical family drama escalation and vague grievances

dialogue.speaker: LeVondrious
dialogue.line: I'm back, and I know what you did.

jokes.text: I hope you saved a slice of that cake for your estranged brother LeVondrious!
jokes.setup: LeVondrious appearing at the wedding
jokes.punchline: His dramatic entrance referencing both cake and their estrangement
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the LeVondrious setup - dramatic entrance with cake priorities

dialogue.speaker: LeVondrious
dialogue.line: I have traveled far and wide, through deserts of despair and oceans of regret, to be here on this momentous day. And I bring with me a seven-tier wedding cake, because I heard there would be cake.

jokes.text: Don't worry. I brought it back.
jokes.setup: LeVondrious referencing 'the microwave incident'
jokes.punchline: Revealing he brought the microwave back to the wedding
jokes.explanation: Absurd resolution to family drama - bringing stolen appliances to a wedding as reconciliation

jokes.text: Well. Now no one gets any popcorn.
jokes.setup: LeVondrious dramatically destroying the microwave
jokes.punchline: Donna's practical concern about popcorn access
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna response - cutting through family drama with practical concerns about snacks

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: Hey, hey, hey! What are you doing? That's the microwave!

dialogue.speaker: LeVondrious
dialogue.line: I'm destroying it!

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: Well, how am I supposed to make my popcorn?

jokes.text: You said you wanted a little drama. That's why I love you, girl!
jokes.setup: April earlier wanting some Meagle drama
jokes.punchline: LeVondrious revealing April orchestrated his appearance
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff revealing April as the puppet master behind the drama - perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: LeVondrious
dialogue.line: Wait, you set this whole thing up? You got me to come here?

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I wanted to see you two go at it. Meagle drama is the best drama.

dialogue.speaker: LeVondrious
dialogue.line: That's... actually kind of genius.

tmdb_overview: Donna and Joe tie the knot, while April attempts to keep the wedding running smoothly. Ron creates an awkward situation between Tom and Lucy. Jen Barkley arrives at Ben and Leslie's house with an interesting opportunity.
insight: Donna's wedding delivers 78 jokes in 22 minutes—highest density since Season 4.
